info.title: Sibiu Restaurant App — AI & Recommendations Contract v1.0
info.description: Supplement to the main API contract. Covers AI-powered recommendations, flash deals engine, and admin AI configuration. Designed to start rule-based and evolve into ML-backed without changing the contract.
info.design_philosophy: Every AI endpoint has a fallback. If the AI service is down, the system returns manual/static recommendations. Frontend never knows the difference.

RecommendationContext.description: What the AI knows when generating a recommendation. Built from session + order history.
previous_orders_in_session: uuid (order IDs, for dessert/drink upsell timing)

Recommendation.reason: string (human-readable, e.g. 'Merge excelent cu grătarul comandat' or 'Recomandat de bucătar')
Recommendation.confidence: number (0.0 to 1.0 — how confident the system is in this rec)

FlashDeal.reason: string (e.g. 'Adaugă o Papanași cu 20% reducere în următoarele 5 minute!')

AdminAIConfig.daily_prompt: string (admin's natural language instruction, e.g. 'Azi avem pește proaspăt, recomandă pastrav cu orice comandă de salată. Avem surplus de tiramisu, pune-l ca flash deal.')
suppressed_items: uuid (menu item IDs to NOT recommend today)

SimilarityRule.is_auto_generated: boolean (true if AI created it from order history)

GET /sessions/:session_id/recommendation.description: Main recommendation endpoint. Called after customer adds items to cart or after placing an order. Returns contextual suggestions: pairings for current items, upsells, and any active flash deal.

query_params.current_item_ids: string (comma-separated menu_item UUIDs currently in cart)

pairings.description: Items that go well with what the customer selected

response_200.flash_deal: FlashDeal | null (only one at a time, only on trigger='order_placed')

notes: Frontend calls this when cart changes and when an order is submitted
Flash deals only appear AFTER ordering, not while browsing
If AI service is unreachable, returns manual pairs_well_with data as fallback
Results are cached per session+cart combo for 60 seconds

POST /sessions/:session_id/flash-deal/:d.description: Customer claims the flash deal before the countdown expires. Creates a new single-item order with the discount applied.

response_201.message: string (e.g. 'Papanași adăugați la comandă cu 20% reducere!')

errors.409: Deal already claimed in this session

GET /sessions/:session_id/flash-deal/act.description: Check if there's an active flash deal for this session. Used on page refresh to restore the countdown timer.

GET /admin/restaurants/:restaurant_id/ai.description: Get today's AI configuration. If none exists, returns defaults.

POST /admin/restaurants/:restaurant_id/a.description: Admin sets today's AI prompt and configuration. Only one per day — POST replaces any existing config for today.

errors.400: Validation error (discount too high, invalid item IDs, etc.)

notes: The daily_prompt is processed by the AI to adjust recommendation weights
MVP: parse the prompt for item names and boost/suppress accordingly
V2: send to LLM to extract structured intents from free-form Romanian text

GET /admin/restaurants/:restaurant_id/ai.description: Past AI configs for review. Admin can see what worked.

GET /admin/restaurants/:restaurant_id/si.description: View all pairing/similarity rules. Mix of manual (admin-created) and auto-generated (from order history patterns).

query_params.source_item_id: uuid (optional, filter rules for a specific item)

POST /admin/restaurants/:restaurant_id/s.description: Admin manually creates a pairing rule.

request.source_category_id: uuid | null (e.g. 'all grătare items pair with this drink')
request.weight: number (0.0 to 1.0, default 0.8 for manual rules)

notes: source_item_id OR source_category_id must be set, not both
Category-level rules apply to ALL items in that category
Example: source_category='Feluri principale', target='Vin roșu casa', relationship='pairs_with'

DELETE /admin/restaurants/:restaurant_id.description: Remove a pairing rule. For auto-generated rules, this teaches the system to not suggest this combo.

POST /admin/restaurants/:restaurant_id/s.description: Trigger auto-generation of similarity rules from order history. Finds items that are frequently ordered together.

request.min_co_occurrence: integer (default 5, how many times items must be ordered together)

notes: MVP: simple co-occurrence counting (items ordered in the same session)
V2: weighted by time proximity, category diversity, repeat customers

REALTIME_AI_EVENTS.description: Additional WebSocket events for AI features, sent on existing WS connections.

flash_deal_available.description: Sent after order is placed and AI decides to offer a deal

flash_deal_expired.description: Countdown hit zero, deal no longer claimable

flash_deal_claimed.description: Staff knows a discounted order is coming in

ai_engine_internals.description: NOT part of the API contract — this documents how the backend AI service works internally so the backend team knows what to build.

recommendation_pipeline.step_1_gather_context: Build RecommendationContext from session, current cart, time, order history
recommendation_pipeline.step_2_apply_admin_config: Check today's AdminAIConfig — boost promoted items, suppress suppressed ones
recommendation_pipeline.step_3_query_similarity_rules: Find rules where source matches any item in cart (item-level first, then category-level fallback)
recommendation_pipeline.step_4_score_candidates: Score each candidate: rule_weight * admin_boost * time_relevance * (1 - already_ordered_penalty)
recommendation_pipeline.step_5_deduplicate: Remove items already in cart or already ordered this session
recommendation_pipeline.step_6_format_response: Top 3 pairings, top 2 upsells, and optionally generate a flash deal

flash_deal_trigger_logic.when: After order_placed, with 30 second delay (let the order confirmation UX breathe)
flash_deal_trigger_logic.what: Pick from: promoted_items in today's config, OR items with tag 'surplus' or 'slow_mover', OR highest-margin item not yet ordered
guard_rails: Max N deals per session (from admin config, default 2)
Never offer a deal on something already ordered
Never offer a deal if session total is under 30 RON (don't discount tiny orders)
Don't trigger on the first order of the session (let them settle in)

mvp_vs_v2.mvp_recommendation: Rule-based: similarity_rules table + admin daily config + basic scoring
mvp_vs_v2.v2_recommendation: Feed daily_prompt to an LLM to extract structured intents; use collaborative filtering on order history; A/B test recommendation strategies
mvp_vs_v2.mvp_flash_deal: Admin configures which items to deal, system picks timing
mvp_vs_v2.v2_flash_deal: ML model predicts which deal maximizes claim rate per customer context
